summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a legendary Fillmore East show highlighted by an epic Dark Star and a standout Not Fade Away/China Cat jam.
review: The recording is a pristine SBD that sounds as fresh today as it did in ’70, with minimal hiss and excellent balance despite minor pitch‑correction quirks noted by a few archivists. Musically the night shines – Dark Star dominates the praise, bolstered by a fiery Not Fade Away featuring an extended China Cat tease, a soaring St. Stephen and memorable Pigpen banter. Overall reviewers are overwhelmingly enthusiastic.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews (mostly 5‑star) extol both the performance and the soundboard quality; only a handful of low or mixed ratings exist, so the combined show (70%) + recording (30%) score is near‑perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with very little noise; some reviewers note slight speed/pitch variations in certain tracks but overall the transfer is superb.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set builds beautifully from the exploratory Dark Star into a high‑energy Not Fade Away/China Cat segment, then lifts into a soaring St. Stephen before winding down with an intimate Lovelight and a raucous Pigpen finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his soaring lead on Dark Star and overall top‑form playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer notes Phil’s bass carries the day during the extended jam; otherwise no specific comments.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob) gets kudos for his rhythmic “groovy” jam in Not Fade Away and his powerful rhythm work later in the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drumming is highlighted, especially his “cannon” fills during Dark Star and St. Stephen.
